__warnings.note: Kept Shona 'Mhoro' verbatim — it's Rwen's signature greeting and is recognised across the app. Reviewer: confirm this stays Mhoro for Korean speakers, or flip to '안녕하세요, {{name}}님!' if user research shows Korean users find Mhoro confusing.
Kept Shona 'Ndine urombo' (I'm sorry) verbatim for the same brand-signature reason. Korean explanation follows. Reviewer: confirm or flip to '죄송해요 —'.
Same Mhoro decision applied. Reviewer: confirm.

Used '{{name}}님' (with honorific 님). Korean convention is to attach 님 to a name when addressing politely. If {{name}} is sometimes a non-Korean Latin-letter name, '님' still attaches naturally. Reviewer: confirm.
'{{lang}} companion · AI' → '{{lang}} 컴패니언 · AI'. Used loanword 컴패니언; alternative '동반자' is warmer but more literary. Reviewer: pick register.
'tap ⏹ to stop' → '⏹를 누르세요'. Polite-form (해요체) used throughout. Reviewer: confirm honorific level matches the rest of the pack.
All 5 locale files default to polite '해요체/합니다체'. Reviewer: confirm this is the right register for an AI-companion app aimed at adult learners (20s-40s). 반말 would feel too casual; 하십시오체 too formal.

input.placeholder_convo_active: 대화가 진행 중이에요 — 말씀하시거나 ⏹를 눌러 멈추세요

messages.welcome: Mhoro, {{name}}님! 오늘은 어떤 이야기를 하고 싶으세요?
messages.error_generic: Ndine urombo — 무언가 잘못됐어요. 다시 시도해 주세요.
messages.auto_listen_greeting: Mhoro! 듣고 있어요. 준비되시면 언제든지 말씀해 주세요.
